globalRules.title: Balanced Budget Requirement
globalRules.description: Arizona Constitution and Title 42 A.R.S. require a balanced, all-inclusive budget. Appropriated resources must equal appropriated expenditures.
globalRules.citation: Ariz. Const. art. IX; A.R.S. Title 42

funds.name: General Fund
funds.description: Discretionary resources for public safety, libraries, parks, and citywide services.

funds.name: HURF & Local Street Sales Tax
funds.description: Highway User Revenue Fund and voter-approved 0.30% Local Street Sales Tax. Both are restricted to street-related expenditures only.
funds.citation: A.R.S. highway user revenue statutes; Mesa LSST ballot measure
funds.alertMessage: HURF and Local Street Sales Tax dollars can only fund street-related work — not libraries, parks, or general public safety.

funds.name: Local Transportation Assistance Fund
funds.description: State lottery (LTAF) and transit fund revenues restricted to transit expenditures.
funds.alertMessage: Transit and LTAF funds must stay in transit services.

funds.name: Public Safety Sales Tax
funds.description: Voter-approved sales tax restricted to public safety programs and projects (2022 GO bond alignment).
funds.citation: Mesa voter-approved Public Safety Sales Tax
funds.alertMessage: Public Safety Sales Tax is voter-dedicated and cannot be reallocated to non-public-safety services.

funds.name: Quality of Life Sales Tax
funds.description: Voter-approved tax supporting up to 120 Police sworn FTE and 65 Fire sworn FTE plus enhanced services. Some flexibility within ballot envelope.
funds.alertMessage: Quality of Life tax must stay within voter-approved public safety and enhanced services purposes.

funds.name: Utility Enterprise Funds
funds.description: Business-like utility operations funded by rates. Revenues must cover utility costs.

funds.name: Utility Fund Contribution to General Government
funds.description: Council resolution caps utility transfer at 30% of operating revenues (25% public safety, 5% general services). FY 2025/26 budgeted at $147M.
funds.citation: Mesa City Council utility contribution resolution
funds.alertMessage: Utility contributions to General Government cannot exceed 30% of utility operating revenues.

funds.name: Environmental Compliance Fee
funds.description: Monthly utility fee funding unfunded EPA and state environmental mandates (~$18.3M).

funds.name: Falcon Field Airport Fund
funds.description: Airport revenues must support airport operations per FAA restrictions. Cannot fund general city purposes.

funds.name: Ambulance Transport Fund
funds.description: Self-sustaining emergency transport program; expanded citywide March 2025.

funds.name: Employee Benefit Trust
funds.description: Self-insured employee and retiree health benefits. City contribution ~$100.5M FY 2025/26.

funds.name: Grant Funds
funds.description: Federal and state grants restricted to designated uses with reporting requirements.

funds.name: Transient Lodging Tax
funds.description: 5% bed tax dedicated to Visit Mesa, convention center, spring training facilities, and related debt.

funds.name: Secondary Property Tax / GO Bonds
funds.description: Secondary property tax ($44.3M levy) pays GO bond debt only. Median home ~$160/year.

transferRules.message: Transportation restricted funds cannot be transferred to General Fund services.
Utility transfer limited to 30% of utility operating revenues.
Falcon Field airport profits cannot fund general city purposes.
